title: fraxinus anomala var. triphylla
intro: Fraxinus anomala var. triphylla, also known as Manchurian ash, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its unique three-leafed branches. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus anomala var. triphylla include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Fraxinus anomala var. triphylla is typically prepared as a tea or infused in water to extract its medicinal compounds, and it can also be used in herbal preparations like tinctures and capsules.
